newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. The June 8 annual shareholder meeting was routine governance, not a catalyst. Last fundamental datapoints remain mid-May: Q1 2026 revenue +59% YoY to $25M, a next-gen design win with a top customer (2027 production), and analyst PT raises (Northland $72, UBS $70). Today's -4.74% to $75.40 has no identifiable fundamental driver and reads as flow-driven consolidation after a +148% YTD run.

sources.label: Ambiq Q1 2026 financial results (company press release)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already cut AMBQ for post-IPO crowding and indirect robotics exposure; no new information since June 6 warrants a second consecutive move. Today's -4.7% flow-driven pullback with the edge-AI thesis intact marginally improves asymmetry, justifying a small positive drift but not a rank change.
verdicts.bull: SPOT-based ultra-low-power edge AI is genuinely scarce silicon for battery-constrained robotics/wearable endpoints, and the confirmed 2027 design win plus 59% YoY growth show real commercialization, not just narrative.
verdicts.neutral: At $1.57B cap and +148% YTD, the rerating has already harvested most of the hidden-alpha discount; the stock is now a known edge-AI name digesting gains, with the next real test at Q2 earnings.
verdicts.bear: Robotics exposure is indirect (sensor hubs and always-on endpoints, not actuation or core compute), the company is still unprofitable until 2027-2028 at best, and a crowded post-IPO register can de-rate fast if growth decelerates.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings and any new named design wins in robotics/wearable endpoints that validate the 2027 production ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-IPO momentum unwind: multiple compression on any growth wobble given +148% YTD and the alpha already pulled forward.
